Our story

Where tradition meets community

Located at 7 Victoria Street in the heart of Hamilton, the Yoga Centre Bermuda has quietly grown into the island's most trusted institution for traditional yoga practice. Founded in 1994 by Joanne Wohlmuth and Frances Marshall (co-founders) — it is Bermuda's first yoga centre.

Our lineage honours practices that have guided seekers for thousands of years: asana, pranayama, meditation, and the exploration of yoga as a spiritual discipline. Our mission is to share the full spectrum of the benefits of yoga, beyond merely the physical, with every individual.

We continue to evolve and have survived decades of radical changes: in the world, on the island, and within these walls. Still we remain firmly rooted, offering the same reliable teachings year-after-year – because the Yoga Centre is a community, not just a studio.

42Years of practice
1stYoga centre in Bermuda
RYTYoga Alliance accredited training

Our evolution

Rooted and still growing

The founders

A Shared Vision

United by devotion to yoga, Joanne and Frances shared a vision: to create a dedicated space where yoga could be accessible to all.

→

1994

The Yoga Centre Opens Its Doors

A sanctuary for practice, learning, and personal transformation is established in the heart of Hamilton — something Bermuda had never seen before.

→

Today

A Living Community

Grounded in its original spirit, the centre today is a thriving collective of teachers and students united by the transformative power of yoga.

"Yoga is not just physical exercise. It is a way of life — a means of finding your true Self and expressing that truth in every moment."

— Sri Swami Satchidananda

Community & events

A space for gathering, healing,
and going deeper

Beyond the weekly schedule, The Yoga Centre hosts workshops, sound therapy, and special events throughout the year.

Upcoming

Events & workshops

Thursday, June 25th · 5:30–7:00pm

Summer Workshop

We Can Still Stand Tall

A workshop focusing on bodily posture — how to work with, strengthen, and improve it, and raise awareness of the common bodily parts that impact how we stand and move. Great for anyone with posture issues or common health issues (neck, shoulder, hip, knee, feet) impacted by how we stand. This workshop will benefit both old and young alike in this computer age of too much sitting!

Themes: Alignment loops, tensegrity, balance for strength and flexibility (stirra and sukka), and muscle groups that support our ability to stand tall.
Cost: $35

Register or enquire

Starting July 15, 2026

Yoga Teacher Training

Summer/Fall Training & Info Session · Yoga on the Rock School of Yoga

A new Yoga Alliance certified 200hr Teacher Training commences July 15th, running until November 15th, 2026. This 4-month accelerated programme is primarily held at The Yoga Centre with some online sessions. Join us for an Info Session on Friday, June 26th at 6:00pm to learn more. A 6-month January–June training is also planned for 2027.

Express interest

A unique offering

Private & Group
Yoga Therapy Sessions

Work one-on-one or in small groups with our C-IAYT Certified Yoga Therapists — trained to use yoga tools as therapy to support your individual health, recovery, and wellbeing goals.

Contact Joanne at yogaontherock@logic.bm or Frances at livingyogabermuda@gmail.com to enquire about Yoga Therapy.

The community

Our teachers

Core faculty

Joanne Wohlmuth

Co-Founder

Co-founder of The Yoga Centre Bermuda, Joanne has been practising and teaching yoga for over 45 years. An E-RYT 500, C-IAYT certified Yoga Therapist, and founder of Yoga on the Rock, she brings a deeply holistic and individual approach to every class.

Full profile

Frances Marshall

Co-Founder

Co-founder of The Yoga Centre Bermuda and founder of LivingYoga Bermuda, Frances is an IAYT Certified Yoga Therapist and RYT 500+. She specialises in multi-level Hatha, Yin, Restorative, and individual Yoga Therapy — with a holistic approach that draws on breathwork, meditation, Ayurveda, and sound healing to support each student's unique journey.

What our community says

Voices from the community

“The Yoga Centre is a highly-qualified, extremely experienced, student-centered yoga studio offering the most authentic traditional yoga in Bermuda. There, my practice has always been calm, peaceful, safe, and indeed, challenging both physically and spiritually. Lessons learned there, I find, I carry with me throughout my life!”

— Tina Nash

“I have taken yoga at other places before, but I love my classes at the Yoga Centre! There, I find the teachers meet me where I am and encourage me to trust my own body without judgement or any feeling of inadequacy. ‘Anyone can do yoga, they remind me!’ The moment I walk up the stairs, I start to calm down. The place has a holy presence, and I can feel it as soon as I enter the door!”

— Laura Mellor

“As golfers we started practicing yoga at the Yoga Centre to secure our backs and up our game of golf. Now we are not only better golfers but also pretty good yoga students too and have graduated in our class from standing on our feet to balancing on our hands and heads too! This has only been possible at the Yoga Centre, where the teachers are first-rate, the class is challenging but safe, and the meditative aspect has been brilliant for our golf score!”

— Heinz Stuhlpfarrer & Dave Ramrattan

“The Yoga Centre for me has always been about community. I started practicing there many years ago, and because of the ambiance there, and the excellent guidance and camaraderie from others, I call the Yoga Centre home. It has been for me a refuge — physically, mentally, and spiritually; and with whatever health challenges I have had, the Yoga Centre has been there to help me heal and rebound. Practicing yoga at the Yoga Centre and particularly with Joanne, has inspired me to answer the call and become a certified yoga teacher also!”

— Beverly-Dawn Knights
